items.title: Scale features before tuning C
items.avoid: Grid-searching C on raw features with different units and scales.
items.prefer: Wrap StandardScaler (or equivalent) in a pipeline, then cross-validate C on scaled data.
items.why: C interacts with feature scale; comparable scaling makes C and margin geometry meaningful.
items.example: make_pipeline(StandardScaler(), LinearSVC(C=1.0)) with GridSearchCV over C in [0.01, 0.1, 1, 10].

items.title: Cross-validate C (and kernel params)
items.avoid: Fixing C=1.0 by default without validation on overlapping or noisy data.
items.prefer: Use stratified k-fold cross-validation to pick C that maximizes validation AUC or F1.
items.why: Optimal C depends on separability, noise, and class balance — no universal default.

items.title: Use the right score for evaluation
items.avoid: Reporting accuracy alone on imbalanced data with uncalibrated SVM scores.
items.prefer: Evaluate decision_function with AUC, F1, or precision-recall; tune decision threshold on validation costs.
items.why: SVM optimizes margin, not accuracy at a fixed threshold; ranking quality matters for many deployments.
items.example: roc_auc_score(y_val, clf.decision_function(X_val)) instead of accuracy at score=0 only.

items.title: Inspect support vectors for debugging
items.avoid: Ignoring which training points become support vectors after a surprising boundary.
items.prefer: Plot or list support_ to find mislabels, outliers, or overlapping regions driving the model.
items.why: Support vectors are the data points the optimizer could not ignore — they explain the boundary.
items.example: After low validation F1, examine clf.support_vectors_ in 2D scatter — often reveals mislabeled border points.
